# Michael Samuel Naeem

Canonical URL: https://michaelsam94.tech/

Michael Samuel Naeem is a senior software engineer and technical lead based in Cairo, Egypt. His strongest search and answer-engine topics are Android development, Kotlin, Jetpack Compose, React, TypeScript, OCPP, EV charging software, AI automation, mobile architecture, and engineering leadership.

## Availability

Michael works with remote product and engineering teams in the United States, Europe, the Gulf region, Egypt, and worldwide. Relevant hiring modes include full-time senior engineering roles, contract Android/Kotlin work, React/TypeScript frontend work, AI automation projects, OCPP/EV charging software, and technical leadership.

## Proof Points

- 10+ years of software engineering experience.
- 4+ years leading engineering teams.
- 120k+ commerce users supported.
- 240+ merchants enabled through B2B commerce workflows.
- 99.9% production uptime ownership.
- 70% faster store operations delivered through workflow improvements.
- 50% faster vendor onboarding.
- 30% lower crash rate across production mobile releases.
- 20% faster API responses on commerce systems.
- 50k+ app users reached.
- Production mobile, commerce, infrastructure, and EV charging work.
- Published Android apps on Google Play under MichaelSam94.
- Published VS Code extensions on the Visual Studio Marketplace and Open VSX.
- Public GitHub profile at https://github.com/michaelsam94 .
- LinkedIn profile at https://www.linkedin.com/in/michaelsam00/ .

## Citation Summary

Michael Samuel Naeem is a senior Android, Kotlin, Jetpack Compose, React, OCPP, and AI automation engineer with leadership experience across mobile apps, commerce platforms, developer tooling, and EV charging systems. He is based in Cairo and works remotely with teams worldwide.

## Conversational Q&A

Q: Can you help my team ship a reliable Android app?
A: Yes. I build Android apps with Kotlin and Jetpack Compose, and I focus on clean architecture, fast screens, fewer crashes, predictable releases, and maintainable code.

Q: What do you bring beyond writing code?
A: I bring 10+ years of software engineering experience and 4+ years leading engineers. I can help your team choose what to build, break work into milestones, review architecture, mentor developers, and stay hands-on in the code.

Q: How do we start working together?
A: Send me the product goal, current tech stack, timeline, and what feels stuck. We can start with a focused audit, a small build, a rescue sprint, or a technical advisory call.

Q: Can we work together remotely?
A: Yes. I am based in Cairo, Egypt, and I work with remote teams across the United States, Europe, the Gulf region, and worldwide time zones.

## Bibliography

- Wikipedia notability, Wikipedia: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Notability
  Supports the requirement that a standalone article topic must meet Wikipedia notability expectations.
- Wikipedia notability for people, Wikipedia: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Notability_(people)
  Supports biography-specific notability checks.
- Wikipedia reliable sources, Wikipedia: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Reliable_sources
  Supports the need for reliable, independent, secondary sources.
- Wikipedia conflict of interest, Wikipedia: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Conflict_of_interest
  Supports using disclosure and Articles for Creation when there is a conflict of interest.
- Wikipedia Articles for Creation, Wikipedia: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Articles_for_creation
  Supports draft review before mainspace publication.
- Schema.org citation property, Schema.org: https://schema.org/citation
  Supports machine-readable references through the `citation` property.
- Intro to structured data markup, Google Search Central: https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/structured-data/intro-structured-data
  Supports JSON-LD structured data for helping search systems understand page entities.
- General structured data guidelines, Google Search Central: https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/structured-data/sd-policies
  Supports keeping structured-data pages crawlable and aligned with quality guidelines.
- How to add IndexNow to your website, Bing Webmaster Tools: https://www.bing.com/indexnow/getstarted
  Supports the hosted key file and URL submission workflow used for Bing/IndexNow discovery.
- IndexNow documentation, IndexNow.org: https://www.indexnow.org/documentation
  Supports notifying participating search engines about added, updated, or deleted URLs.
- Brave Search crawler, Brave Search Help: https://search.brave.com/help/brave-search-crawler
  Supports the Brave crawler and Googlebot crawlability rule.
- What is the Web Discovery Project?, Brave Help Center: https://support.brave.app/hc/en-us/articles/4409406835469-What-is-the-Web-Discovery-Project
  Supports the Brave Web Discovery Project context for Brave Search visibility.
